Tests for TypeError exceptions when RegExp methods are called with invalid arguments.

# Purpose
This code is a test script that verifies the behavior of certain `RegExp` prototype methods when they are called with an incorrect argument type. It specifically tests the `test`, `exec`, and `toString` methods of `RegExp.prototype` by attempting to invoke them with `ReferenceError` as an argument. Each method call is wrapped in a `try-catch` block to handle exceptions. The script asserts that a `TypeError` is thrown in each case, confirming that these methods do not accept a `ReferenceError` object as a valid argument. This ensures that the methods behave as expected when given inappropriate input.
